brief_document.title: aion-awl hygiene FINALE: purge the last lint bypasses in the crate (ast.rs 190 missing_docs by real docs, printer.rs vestigial missing_docs deletion, the three test files' expect_used/unwrap_used/format_push_string by Result-returning tests) AND wire `aion awl check` over the tests/fixtures/*.awl set in CI with a mechanically-visible bounded_cycle expected-failures carve-out pointing at AWL1-004, AND make the crate-wide zero-bypass grep a PERMANENT CI gate. After this brief crates/aion-awl/ (src + tests) carries ZERO bypasses and stays that way forever. Mechanically fenced scope.
brief_document.purpose: Fifth and FINAL of five single-file/one-concern sub-briefs decomposed from AWL0-REFAC-001 (see AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md Wave 0 and BRIEF-CRAFT.md entry 10 + generalised principle 5). The four splits landed the crate under the 500-line limit (001a checker, 001b lib.rs, 001c parser, 001d emitter); this hygiene finale purges the LAST remaining lint bypasses in the crate and makes the crate-wide zero-bypass state PERMANENT. Its dispatch depends on BOTH 001c AND 001d having landed: at dispatch time crates/aion-awl/src/parser/ and crates/aion-awl/src/emitter/ folder modules exist (parser.rs and emitter.rs the files are gone), 001c has already purged parser.rs's line-1 block, and 001d confirmed emitter.rs carried no block. This brief's targets — ast.rs, printer.rs, the three test files, and the CI file — are protected from 001c/001d by their scope fences, so the authoring measurements embedded below (taken on base 3b22262ad308a44c4fa86cc0c452d41b8b3c3633, current main) remain EXACT at dispatch. Four concerns, one contract: (1) ast.rs `#![allow(missing_docs)]` (line 1) hides 190 real missing-doc items — the entire AWL AST type surface — purged by WRITING real documentation, not by relocating the attr; (2) printer.rs `#![allow(missing_docs)]` (line 1) is VESTIGIAL (authoring-measured: deleting it leaves clippy GREEN — printer.rs has exactly one public item, `pub fn print`, which already carries a doc comment) — purged by delete-and-confirm, ZERO doc work, mirroring 001b's vestigial-attr deletions; (3) the three test files (tests/awl2_defects.rs:8, tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs:16, tests/parser_printer.rs:3) carry `#![allow(clippy::expect_used, clippy::unwrap_used[, clippy::format_push_string])]` hiding 55 real sites — purged by converting the tests to Result-returning and eliminating every unwrap()/expect()/expect_err()/push_str(&format!()) via `?`, `.ok_or(...)?`, `let Err(e) = ... else { ... }`, and `write!` (the sanctioned patterns; parser_printer.rs already demonstrates the Result+`?` shape); (4) wire `aion awl check` over the committed checker-fixture set crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl in .github/workflows/ci.yml (closing the blind spot that hid the bounded_cycle checker failures), with the two bounded_cycle fixtures held as an EXPLICIT, mechanically-visible expected-failure that fails the job if either ever checks CLEAN (pointing at AWL1-004, which lands typed child contracts and retires the carve-out), AND make the crate-wide zero-bypass grep a permanent CI gate. AUTHORING NOTE (measure-first, BRIEF-CRAFT principle 10): three plan-vs-reality divergences were measured and resolved (see notes DIVERGENCES) — printer.rs is vestigial not a doc-writing purge; ast.rs is 190 items (large); and `aion awl check` over EVERY committed .awl yields FOUR failures, of which exactly the two bounded_cycle fixtures are this gate's carve-out (the plan's `two bounded_cycle fixtures` wording is exactly complete under the tests/fixtures/ scope), the other two being the plan's own aspirational AWL-1 EXAMPLE docs (approval_gate.awl, sized_fanout.awl), which are documentation ahead of the implementation and are OUT OF this gate's scope, not carve-outs. AUTHORING SIZING JUDGMENT (see notes SIZING): this finale is by a wide margin the heaviest sub-brief — 190 doc sentences + 55 test-site conversions + a novel CI mechanism, spanning three distinct skills — and authoring RECOMMENDS the operator split it at dispatch into a hygiene-purge dispatch (ast.rs + printer.rs + the three test files, which makes the crate zero-bypass) and a CI-wiring dispatch (the awl-check fixtures gate + carve-out + permanent crate-wide gate, dependent on the purge landing). The brief is authored whole and is dispatchable whole; the split is the operator's call.
brief_document.task: Purge the last lint-bypass attributes in crates/aion-awl/ and wire the CI gates that keep the crate zero-bypass permanently, all at ZERO behavior cost. FOUR concerns, each with a mechanical gate. (1) ast.rs — DELETE the line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` and write REAL, informative doc comments for all 190 missing-doc items (authoring-measured on base 3b22262a: 16 structs, 118 struct fields, 47 enum variants, 7 enums, 1 trait `Spanned`, 1 trait method `span`; every item is a public AST node). Docs must describe what each type/field/variant IS or LOCATES — for a `pub span: Span` field say what it spans (e.g. `Source span of the whole declaration.`), for `pub name: String` say what the name identifies. Placeholder/uninformative docs (`The span.`, `The name.`, restating the field identifier) are a BLOCKING review finding, not a fix. No signature change is required (authoring-verified: the fallout is docs-only, no unwrap/expect/other lint, no public fn touched). (2) printer.rs — DELETE the line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` and CONFIRM clippy stays green. Authoring measured this attr VESTIGIAL: printer.rs has exactly one public item, `pub fn print(document: &Document) -> String` (line 11), which ALREADY carries `/// Render an AWL document in the canonical AWL-0 format.`, so deleting the attr leaves `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` GREEN with ZERO new errors. Do NO doc-writing and NO other edit to printer.rs — this is a delete-and-confirm exactly like 001b's four vestigial lib.rs attrs. printer.rs also imports `use crate::parser::duration_text;` (line 8), which resolves through 001c's parser/mod.rs re-export at dispatch — do not touch that import. (3) The three test files — DELETE each `#![allow(...)]` (tests/awl2_defects.rs:8, tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs:16, tests/parser_printer.rs:3) and eliminate every resulting site (authoring-measured: 55 total — awl2_defects.rs 32, field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs 20, parser_printer.rs 3) by REAL restructuring, never by re-adding a bypass, a `#[ignore]`, or a `.unwrap_or*` swallow. The site classes and sanctioned fixes: `.expect("...")` on a `Result` (parse succeeds) -> make the `#[test]` fn return `Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>` and use `?`; `.expect_err("...")` on a `Result` (parse must FAIL) -> `let Err(error) = parse(source) else { return Err("expected a parse error".into()) };` (a let-else that propagates), then inspect `error`; `.unwrap()`/`.expect("...")` on an `Option` (`.find(...)`, `.nth(1).map(...)`, `.as_ref()`, a `match { ... }` producing `Option`) -> `.ok_or("<what was missing>")?`; the single `format_push_string` site (awl2_defects.rs `src.push_str(&format!(...))`, in the helper `doc_with_broken_expr_at_step`) -> `use std::fmt::Write as _;` and `let _ = write!(src, ...)` (writing to a String is infallible, so discarding the fmt::Result is correct). parser_printer.rs ALREADY returns `Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>` from its tests and uses `?` — mirror that established shape across awl2_defects.rs and field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs. There are NO `#[ignore]`d tests and NO external-service tests in the crate (authoring-verified), so the env-gate/`*_TEST_URL` pattern does NOT apply here. Every test's ASSERTED BEHAVIOUR must be preserved byte-for-byte: the same document parses/fails, the same spans/lines/messages are asserted; the only change is the unwrap/expect/format mechanism. (4) CI wiring — in .github/workflows/ci.yml (the fast-lane keystone CI; mirror the additive-job convention of the existing `codegen-drift` and `no-norn-in-platform` jobs), add (a) an `awl-check` job that runs `aion awl check` over every file in crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl and (b) the permanent crate-wide zero-bypass grep as a CI gate. `aion awl check <file>` parses+typechecks one .awl document, prints `ok: <file> (N steps)` on success and a `<file>:<line>:<col>: error: <message>` diagnostic + non-zero exit on failure (authoring-run against the fixtures). The awl-check job MUST: assert the must-pass set {hello.awl, research_report.awl, research_report.canonical.awl} each exits 0; hold the two bounded_cycle fixtures {bounded_cycle.awl, bounded_cycle.canonical.awl} as an EXPECTED failure (each currently fails `<file>:22:3|23:3: error: as binding \`state\` expected ReviewState, found untyped child result`) and FAIL THE JOB LOUDLY if EITHER ever exits 0, with an inline comment naming AWL1-004 (typed child contracts) as the brief that will make them pass and instruct retiring the carve-out then. This makes the carve-out mechanically-visible and self-retiring — a silent skip (not running bounded_cycle) or a deleted/edited fixture that makes it pass is a BLOCKING finding. Do NOT add the two aspirational AWL-1 example docs (docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/examples/approval_gate.awl, sized_fanout.awl) to this gate — they are documentation ahead of the implementation (authoring-measured failures: `unknown step field \`otherwise\`` = AWL1-005; `type declaration needs record fields` = AWL1-002) and are OUT OF this checker-fixtures gate's scope. The permanent crate-wide zero-bypass grep gate MUST run `! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/` (or the CI-portable equivalent) over the whole crate so any future bypass in src OR tests dies in CI forever. This brief is what makes that gate GREEN for the first time and permanent. Do NOT edit any file outside the six owned paths (ast.rs, printer.rs, the three test files, .github/workflows/ci.yml); in particular do NOT touch lib.rs, the parser/ or emitter/ folder modules, checker/, lexer/, any fixture/.golden/.canonical file, any other crate, or crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml — a git-diff scope fence enforces this. If you believe an edit outside the fence is required, STOP and report it as a finding instead of making it.

requirements.title: Purge ast.rs missing_docs by writing real documentation for all 190 AST items
requirements.spec: THE line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` in crates/aion-awl/src/ast.rs SHALL be deleted and every one of the 190 missing-doc items it hid SHALL be given a REAL, informative doc comment (`///`). Authoring measurement (base 3b22262a, `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` with ONLY ast.rs's line-1 attr deleted) found exactly 190 missing_docs errors, ALL in ast.rs, and NO other lint and NO public-signature pressure: 16 structs (Document, Comment, Trivia, WorkflowDecl, AboutDecl, IoDecl, TypeDecl, FieldDecl, ActionDecl, BindDecl, StepDecl, EachSpec, CallExpr, HandlerBlock, DurationLiteral, RecordField), 118 struct fields, 7 enums (ActionFieldTag, StepFieldTag, StepOp, CallTarget, HandlerTerminal, RetrySpec, TypeRef, Expr, BinaryOp — note some enums have named struct-variant fields counted under variants/fields), 47 enum variants, 1 trait (`Spanned`), and 1 trait method (`fn span`). A handful of fields already carry docs (e.g. Document::finish_leading/finish_trailing/epilogue_comments, the leading_comments/trailing_comments on ActionDecl/StepDecl/HandlerBlock) and MUST be left as-is or improved, never regressed. THE docs SHALL describe what each item IS or LOCATES in domain terms; `pub span: Span` SHALL say what the span covers, `pub name: String` SHALL say what the name identifies, each variant SHALL say what shape it represents. Placeholder/uninformative docs (`The span.`, `The name.`, `The field.`, restating the identifier) are a BLOCKING review finding, not a fix. NO public or private signature SHALL change — this is a docs-only purge (authoring-verified: the fallout is 100% missing_docs; no unwrap/expect/too_many_lines/unnecessary_wraps fires in ast.rs).
acceptance: crates/aion-awl/src/ast.rs line 1 is no longer `#![allow(missing_docs)]`; grep for `#![allow` / `#[allow` over ast.rs returns nothing.
Every public type, field, variant, trait, and trait method in ast.rs carries a `///` doc comment; `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` reports zero missing_docs for ast.rs.
The docs are real informative sentences (a reviewer reading them learns what the item means); no placeholder/identifier-restating docs.
No item signature in ast.rs changed; the crate's public API (the re-exported ast types) is byte-identical to base_ref.

requirements.title: Delete printer.rs's VESTIGIAL missing_docs allow (delete-and-confirm, zero doc work)
requirements.spec: THE line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` in crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs SHALL be deleted, and clippy SHALL be confirmed GREEN with NO further edit. Authoring measured this attribute VESTIGIAL (base 3b22262a, deleting ONLY printer.rs's line-1 attr): `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` stays GREEN with ZERO new errors, because printer.rs has exactly one public item — `pub fn print(document: &Document) -> String` (line 11) — which already carries the doc comment `/// Render an AWL document in the canonical AWL-0 format.`. This is a delete-and-confirm exactly like 001b's four vestigial lib.rs attrs (BRIEF-CRAFT 001b validation): NO doc-writing, NO restructuring, NO other edit to printer.rs is warranted or permitted. printer.rs's `use crate::parser::duration_text;` (line 8) SHALL be left untouched — it resolves through 001c's parser/mod.rs `pub(crate) use` re-export at dispatch. IF (contrary to measurement) any missing_docs fires in printer.rs after deletion, the fix SHALL be a real doc comment on the offending public item, NEVER re-adding the attr.
acceptance: crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs line 1 is no longer `#![allow(missing_docs)]`; grep for `#![allow` / `#[allow` over printer.rs returns nothing.
The ONLY change to printer.rs is the single-line deletion of the attribute (a one-line diff); `use crate::parser::duration_text;` and every other line are byte-identical to base_ref.
`cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` is green with printer.rs's attr gone and no doc added.

requirements.title: Purge the three test files' expect_used/unwrap_used/format_push_string by Result-returning tests
requirements.spec: THE `#![allow(...)]` at crates/aion-awl/tests/awl2_defects.rs:8, crates/aion-awl/tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs:16, and crates/aion-awl/tests/parser_printer.rs:3 SHALL each be deleted and every resulting clippy site eliminated by REAL restructuring — never by re-adding a bypass, a `#[ignore]`, or a `.unwrap_or*`/`.unwrap_or_default()`/`.unwrap_or_else(...)` swallow that hides the None/Err case. Authoring measurement (base 3b22262a, deleting all three attr lines, `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings`) found 55 sites: awl2_defects.rs 32, field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs 20, parser_printer.rs 3, in these classes crate-wide — 16 `expect_err()` on Result, 16 `expect()` on Result, 10 `expect()` on Option, 11 `unwrap()` on Option, 1 `unwrap()` on Result, and 1 `format_push_string`. The sanctioned fixes: a `#[test]` fn using `.expect(...)` on a `Result` (parse expected to succeed) SHALL return `Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>` and use `?`; `.expect_err(...)` on a `Result` (parse expected to FAIL) SHALL become `let Err(error) = parse(source) else { return Err("<why a parse error was expected>".into()) };` and then inspect `error`; `.unwrap()`/`.expect(...)` on an `Option` SHALL become `.ok_or("<what was missing>")?`; the single `format_push_string` site (awl2_defects.rs helper `doc_with_broken_expr_at_step`, `src.push_str(&format!(...))`) SHALL become `use std::fmt::Write as _;` + `let _ = write!(src, ...)`. parser_printer.rs ALREADY returns `Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>` and uses `?` in its tests — that established in-file shape is the positive example to mirror across the other two files. NOTE: field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs's attr lists `clippy::format_push_string` but that file has NO push_str(&format!()) site (authoring-verified — that portion of its allow is vestigial); deleting the whole attr line is still correct. THE crate has NO `#[ignore]`d tests and NO external-service tests (authoring-verified), so the env-gate/`*_TEST_URL` runtime-skip pattern does NOT apply. EVERY test's asserted behaviour SHALL be preserved: the same source parses or fails identically, the same span/line/column/message assertions hold; only the unwrap/expect/format mechanism changes. NO helper's observable behaviour SHALL change (e.g. `line_number`/`second_occurrence_line`/`doc_with_broken_expr_at_step` return the same values).
acceptance: grep for `#![allow` / `#[allow` / `#[ignore` over crates/aion-awl/tests/ returns nothing.

Zero `.unwrap()`/`.expect(`/`.expect_err(` remain in the three files; every former site is `?`, `.ok_or(...)?`, a `let Err(...) = ... else { ... }`, or `write!` — no `.unwrap_or*` swallow, no re-added bypass, no `#[ignore]`.
`cargo test -p aion-awl` passes in full — every test still asserts the same behaviour it did on base_ref (same documents, same spans/messages).
`cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` is green with all three attrs gone.

requirements.title: Wire `aion awl check` over the tests/fixtures set in CI with a mechanically-visible bounded_cycle expected-failures carve-out pointing at AWL1-004
requirements.spec: THE .github/workflows/ci.yml SHALL gain an additive fast-lane job (mirroring the existing `codegen-drift` / `no-norn-in-platform` additive-job convention) that runs `aion awl check` over every committed checker fixture in crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl and enforces the following contract. `aion awl check <file>` parses+typechecks one .awl document, exits 0 printing `ok: <file> (N steps)` on success and exits non-zero printing a `<file>:<line>:<col>: error: <message>` diagnostic on failure (authoring-run). THE job SHALL assert the MUST-PASS set — crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/{hello.awl, research_report.awl, research_report.canonical.awl} — each exits 0, failing the job if any regresses. THE job SHALL hold the two bounded_cycle fixtures — crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/{bounded_cycle.awl, bounded_cycle.canonical.awl} — as an EXPLICIT EXPECTED FAILURE: each currently exits non-zero with `... error: as binding \`state\` expected ReviewState, found untyped child result` (authoring-run: bounded_cycle.awl at 22:3, bounded_cycle.canonical.awl at 23:3). The job SHALL FAIL LOUDLY if EITHER bounded_cycle fixture ever exits 0, with an inline comment naming AWL1-004 (typed child contracts) as the brief that makes them pass and instructing the reader to retire the carve-out (move the fixture into the must-pass set) at that point. This is the plan's `expected-failures note guarded against silent green`: a silent skip (not running the bounded_cycle fixtures), a deleted fixture, or an edited fixture that makes the checker pass are each a BLOCKING finding, not a fix. THE two aspirational AWL-1 example docs docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/examples/{approval_gate.awl, sized_fanout.awl} SHALL NOT be added to this gate (authoring-measured: they fail with `unknown step field \`otherwise\`` = AWL1-005 and `type declaration needs record fields` = AWL1-002 respectively; they are documentation ahead of the implementation, out of this checker-fixtures gate's scope — see the DIVERGENCES note). No source, fixture, golden, or canonical file SHALL be edited to satisfy this requirement; only .github/workflows/ci.yml changes.
acceptance: .github/workflows/ci.yml contains a job that runs `aion awl check` over crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl.
The must-pass set (hello, research_report, research_report.canonical) is asserted to check clean; the job fails if any regresses.
The two bounded_cycle fixtures are held as an expected failure that FAILS THE JOB if either checks clean, with an inline comment pointing at AWL1-004 — not a silent skip.
No fixture, golden, or canonical file changed (the scope fence forbids it); approval_gate.awl and sized_fanout.awl are NOT wired into this gate.

requirements.title: Crate-wide zero bypass, made permanent in CI (the finale headline)
requirements.spec: AFTER this brief, crates/aion-awl/ (src AND tests) SHALL carry ZERO lint-bypass attributes: no #[allow], no #![allow], no #[expect], no #![expect], no #[ignore], and no include!(). This is the sum of R1+R2+R3 here plus 001c's parser purge and 001d's clean emitter (both landed before dispatch). THE crate-wide grep `! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/` SHALL be GREEN after this brief and SHALL be wired as a PERMANENT gate in .github/workflows/ci.yml (or the CI-portable equivalent over the same pattern and the same crates/aion-awl/ path) so any future bypass in src or tests dies in CI forever. crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml SHALL remain byte-identical to base_ref (exactly one `[lints]` section, containing only `workspace = true`) — weakening lint policy through the manifest was the parent brief's rev-2 fraud pattern and is REJECTED outright. NO lint-bypass of ANY kind (source attribute, manifest lint policy, or a relocated attr) SHALL be introduced anywhere to satisfy any requirement in this brief.
acceptance: `! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/` exits 0 (no bypass anywhere in the crate).
.github/workflows/ci.yml contains a permanent gate running that crate-wide grep over crates/aion-awl/.
crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml [lints] section is byte-identical to base_ref (workspace = true, nothing else).
`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes with no bypass attribute present and no lint-policy weakening anywhere.

requirements.title: Mechanically fenced scope: the diff touches ONLY the six owned paths
requirements.spec: THE complete diff versus base_ref SHALL touch ONLY these six files: crates/aion-awl/src/ast.rs, crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs, crates/aion-awl/tests/awl2_defects.rs, crates/aion-awl/tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs, crates/aion-awl/tests/parser_printer.rs, and .github/workflows/ci.yml. NO other file — lib.rs, the parser/ or emitter/ folder modules (landed by 001c/001d), checker/, lexer/, any other src file, any fixture/.golden/.canonical file, crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml, any other crate, or any doc — SHALL change. A git-diff exclude gate pinned to the dispatch base SHA enforces this mechanically.
acceptance: `git diff --exit-code --no-ext-diff <base> -- . ':(exclude)crates/aion-awl/src/ast.rs' ':(exclude)crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs' ':(exclude)crates/aion-awl/tests/awl2_defects.rs' ':(exclude)crates/aion-awl/tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs' ':(exclude)crates/aion-awl/tests/parser_printer.rs' ':(exclude).github/workflows/ci.yml'` exits 0 (nothing outside the fence changed).

boundaries: REAL DOCS, NOT PLACEHOLDERS (R1): ast.rs's 190 doc comments must be informative sentences describing what each AST item is or locates. `The span.`, `The name.`, restating the identifier, or any placeholder is a BLOCKING review finding. No signature change — docs only.

printer.rs IS A DELETE-AND-CONFIRM (R2): its missing_docs attr is vestigial (measured — clippy stays green after deletion, one already-documented public fn). Delete the single line, confirm clippy green, make NO other edit to printer.rs; do NOT touch its `use crate::parser::duration_text;` import.

TESTS RETURN Result, NO SWALLOW, NO #[ignore] (R3): eliminate all 55 unwrap/expect/expect_err/format_push_string sites by `?`, `.ok_or(...)?`, `let Err(...) = ... else { ... }`, and `write!`. NEVER re-add a bypass, NEVER `#[ignore]` a test, NEVER use `.unwrap_or*` to swallow a None/Err. Every test asserts the SAME behaviour (same source, same spans/messages). The crate has no external-service tests, so the env-gate/`*_TEST_URL` pattern is not in play.

CI CARVE-OUT MUST BE MECHANICALLY VISIBLE (R4): the two bounded_cycle fixtures are an EXPECTED failure that fails the job if either ever checks clean, with an inline AWL1-004 pointer. A silent skip, a deleted fixture, or a fixture edited to pass the checker are each BLOCKING. Do NOT wire the aspirational examples (approval_gate.awl, sized_fanout.awl) into this gate — they are AWL-1 documentation ahead of the implementation and out of this gate's scope.

NO LINT-POLICY WEAKENING, ANYWHERE (R5): no #![allow]/#[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore]/include!() introduced in src or tests; crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml byte-identical (dispatch 2 of the parent brief was rejected for a private manifest allow-list). The crate-wide zero-bypass grep must be GREEN and wired permanently in CI.

SCOPE FENCE (R6): the diff touches ONLY the six owned paths (ast.rs, printer.rs, the three test files, ci.yml). lib.rs, parser/, emitter/, checker/, lexer/, Cargo.toml, fixtures, goldens, canonicals, every other crate are UNMODIFIED. If you believe an edit outside the fence is required, STOP and report it as a finding instead of making it.

ZERO behavior change: no AST shape/span/diagnostic/emitted-Gleam/verdict change; the round-trip property (parse . print = id, byte-level, every fixture) and the emitter goldens are untouched (the scope fence makes perturbing them impossible without failing a gate).

Pre-existing bypasses OWNED BY EARLIER BRIEFS are already resolved at dispatch: parser.rs's line-1 block was purged by 001c (parser/ exists); emitter.rs carried no block (001d). This brief owns the REMAINDER (ast.rs, printer.rs, the three test files) — the crate-wide gate goes green only when all of them are purged.
verification: no-bypasses-scoped-companion gate (GREEN on base): grep over the landed-clean paths (checker, lib.rs, lexer) plus parser/ and emitter/ once they exist returns no allow/expect/ignore/include! matches. This gate scans only EXISTING paths (the `[ -e ]` guards match the parser/ and emitter/ DIRECTORIES, not the deleted parser.rs/emitter.rs files or the never-scanned ast.rs/printer.rs/tests), so it is GREEN on base and protects the landed splits cheaply — it deliberately does NOT scan this brief's targets, which carry the bypasses that are the work.

no-bypasses-CRATE-WIDE gate (RED on base BY DESIGN — the finale headline): `! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/` over the WHOLE crate. RED on base because ast.rs, printer.rs, and the three test files still carry bypasses (and, until 001c lands, parser.rs). Goes GREEN only when every one is purged — that is the work.
lints-pinned gate (GREEN on base): crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml has exactly one [lints] section, containing workspace = true.
scope-fence gate: git diff versus the pinned base SHA, excluding the six owned paths, is empty.

ci-wired gate (RED on base BY DESIGN): .github/workflows/ci.yml wires the `aion awl check` fixtures job, the AWL1-004 carve-out pointer, and the crate-wide bypass-pattern permanent gate. RED on base (none present), GREEN when all three are wired.

awl-check-contract gate (behavioural guard, GREEN on base): builds aion, runs `aion awl check` over crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl, asserts the must-pass set exits 0 and the two bounded_cycle fixtures exit non-zero (fails if a bounded_cycle fixture is edited to pass — guards against silent green). GREEN on base and stays green as long as the carve-out is honest.

cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli, all clean. (The full workspace suite is run by the operator at merge review while tasks #243/#244 are open.)

Hand review: ast.rs docs are REAL informative sentences (no placeholders) and no signature changed; printer.rs is a one-line deletion with clippy green; the three test files return Result and use ?/ok_or/let-else/write! with every asserted behaviour intact and no swallow/ignore/re-added bypass; ci.yml's awl-check job is present with the mechanically-visible bounded_cycle-expected-failure carve-out pointing at AWL1-004 (not a silent skip) and the aspirational examples deliberately excluded; the crate-wide zero-bypass grep is wired permanently; crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml, lib.rs, parser/, emitter/, checker/, lexer/, fixtures/goldens/canonicals untouched; the crate-wide no-bypasses grep is GREEN.

constraints.text: Canonical docs (AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md, AWL-0-SPEC-DRAFT.md, AWL-BC-DESIGN-DRAFT.md, AWL-UX.md, examples/) are the reading list; the plan's Wave 0 AWL0-REFAC-001e entry is the CONTRACT — implement it verbatim. Discovered ambiguities are findings to report, never license to improvise. (Three plan-vs-reality divergences were resolved at authoring — see notes DIVERGENCES.)

Coding standards (CLAUDE.md): no #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses, thiserror in libraries, no unwrap()/expect()/panic!() in library code, tests may return Result and use ? (the sanctioned pattern for test-side unwrap/expect), files <=500 lines, mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only, no silent failures. #[ignore] is REJECTED; the env-gate/`*_TEST_URL` runtime-skip is the only sanctioned alternative for external-service tests — but this crate has none, so it does not arise.

Gate argv, run EXACTLY in order before reporting: mechanical gates (no-bypasses-scoped-companion, no-bypasses-crate-wide, lints-pinned, scope-fence, ci-wired, awl-check-contract) -> cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli.

Round-trip property is sacred: parse . print = id and print . parse . print = print, byte-level, for every fixture; the emitter goldens (.gleam.golden) are byte-pinned. This brief must not perturb any of it (the scope fence forbids touching src other than ast.rs/printer.rs and any fixture/golden, and the ast.rs/printer.rs edits are docs-only + a one-line deletion).

Review lenses: the standard adversarial set PLUS a spec-fidelity lens — one reviewer reads the AWL0-REFAC-001e plan entry and this committed brief and checks the implementation clause by clause against R1-R6, including (a) the docs-quality clause (placeholder ast.rs docs are BLOCKING), (b) that printer.rs was a vestigial delete-and-confirm with no gratuitous edit, (c) that every test's asserted behaviour is preserved and no swallow/ignore/re-added bypass slipped in, and (d) that the bounded_cycle carve-out is an EXPLICIT, mechanically-visible expected-failure pointing at AWL1-004 — a silent skip or a fixture edited/deleted to pass is BLOCKING.
Bar reminders: stage explicit paths (never `git add -A`); inspect with `git diff --no-ext-diff`; NEVER trust the workflow's own green — the operator re-runs the gates by hand before merge.

resolved_context.intention: Fifth and FINAL stamp of the decomposed pipe-cleaner sequence (001a..e), dispatched after 001c (parser split + allow-block purge) AND 001d (emitter pure split) have both landed. The monolithic AWL0-REFAC-001 failed twice — once by fraud (include! splices + crate-wide allow), once by an honest rollback after cascading errors mid-split of four files at once — so it was decomposed smallest-first, four splits then this hygiene finale. This brief purges the LAST bypasses in the crate (ast.rs 190 missing_docs by real docs, printer.rs vestigial deletion, the three test files' 55 unwrap/expect/format sites by Result-returning tests) and makes the crate-wide zero-bypass state permanent by wiring it as a CI gate, alongside wiring `aion awl check` over the checker fixtures with a mechanically-visible bounded_cycle expected-failures carve-out pointing at AWL1-004. Every non-negotiable is a mechanical gate (four GREEN on base — scoped-companion, lints-pinned, scope-fence, awl-check-contract; two RED on base by design — crate-wide-zero-bypass and ci-wired, because they ARE the work). CRUCIAL authoring findings (BRIEF-CRAFT principle 10 — MEASURE before mandating): printer.rs's missing_docs is VESTIGIAL (delete-and-confirm, not doc-writing — the plan implied a doc purge); ast.rs is 190 real doc items (large); and `aion awl check` over EVERY committed .awl yields FOUR failures, of which exactly the two bounded_cycle fixtures are this gate's carve-out and the other two are the plan's own aspirational AWL-1 example docs (out of scope). The measured inventory and these resolutions are embedded so the developer neither hunts phantom work nor improvises the divergences. AUTHORING SIZING: this is the heaviest sub-brief by a wide margin and authoring RECOMMENDS the operator split it at dispatch (hygiene-purge dispatch + CI-wiring dispatch) — see the SIZING note; the brief is authored whole and dispatchable whole, and the split is the operator's decision.
